entries.baseline: Earlier routing cases identified expected primitives and failure modes, but did not require each case to name the productive outcome or the evidence a good run must leave behind.

evidence: Routing evals are limited to primitive selection, forbidden fallbacks, recovery behavior, productive outcomes, and verification evidence.
Daily-driver cases cover active-goal continuation, source-graph ownership, branch and PR delivery, CI triage, plugin install recovery, and production-sharing audit gates.
source/schemas/evals/routing-cases.schema.json requires productiveOutcome and verificationEvidence for schemaVersion 2 cases.
source/schemas/evals/routing-replay.schema.json defines provider-neutral replay observations for scored routing behavior.
source/tools/validate-source-graph.mjs validates routing case schemaVersion, schema links, existing expected primitives, sanitized strings, and non-empty evidence fields.
source/tools/run-routing-evals.mjs scores golden observations against exact case contracts, fixture-declared required cases, and explicit full-corpus coverage mode.
The cases are sanitized from local usage patterns and current repository contracts rather than copied from first-party provider material.
Evidence references use source paths, memory-index summaries, and validation gates rather than raw transcripts, private absolute paths, or installed caches.
The eval family is source evidence and does not require runtime-link activation before marketplace publication.
The replay runner reads checked-in source and fixture data only; it does not install hooks, mutate provider caches, or write runtime targets.

residualRisks: The checked-in golden replay now covers every routing case; future routing cases should add replay observations in the same change.
Live model sessions should still be monitored for drift against these cases.
Additional team-specific observations should be added as real users hit new routing misses or bypasses.
entries.nextAction: Use the default replay runner as the daily-driver production source gate and node source/tools/run-routing-evals.mjs --require-all-observed before claiming full-corpus routing readiness.

entries.baseline: Kotlin Engineering had source primitives, a referential plugin manifest, and routing cases, but the plugin family did not yet have a durable production-readiness audit tying composition, provenance, validation, and residual rollout risks together.

evidence: source/plugins/kotlin-engineering/plugin.json scopes the plugin to typed Kotlin workflow, Kast-backed navigation, focused review, Gradle proof, and Kotlin-specific hooks.
The plugin metadata explicitly excludes non-Kotlin repositories, generic Git/CI delivery, API-only contract governance, and platform marketplace authoring work.
source/profiles/kotlin-repo-default.json composes kotlin-engineering with engineering-baseline and git-ci-operations instead of moving generic delivery ownership into the Kotlin plugin.
source/tools/validate-source-graph.mjs validates plugin references, hook dependencies, profile duplicate ownership, audit target paths, and routing case expected primitives.
source/tools/validate-source-graph.mjs validates garden/manifests/runtime-links.json source paths, approval boundaries, and Codex marketplace-import command shape.

source/evals/routing/kotlin-engineering-workflows.json records Kotlin-specific route expectations for invariant work, Gradle failure diagnosis, negative capability proof, and companion delivery boundaries.
source/evals/routing/fixtures/golden-routing-observations.json covers the Kotlin Engineering routing cases through the full-corpus replay gate.
source/evals/plugin-benchmarks/kotlin-engineering.json defines real Codex benchmark scenarios for Kotlin Engineering and links each scenario back to an existing routing case and expected primitive.
source/skills/kotlin-gradle-validation now owns the script-backed Gradle runner and JUnit, JaCoCo, and Kotlin build report parsers, removing the older duplicate Gradle-loop owner from active source.
source/skills/negative-capability-proof now includes a proof-record helper that validates red and green proof phases before completion.

garden/manifests/promotions.json records kotlin-engineering as a promoted referential composition and records the bundled Kotlin skills, review agents, hooks, concepts, and kotlin-repo-default profile.
The plugin manifest references canonical source primitives by path instead of copying primitive payloads into source/plugins/kotlin-engineering.
The canonical primitive names are local Kotlin workflow names and do not collide with first-party system skill names in this repository.
Kotlin Engineering is exposed through source marketplace composition and does not require direct writes to runtime targets in this audit.
garden/manifests/runtime-links.json records an approval-gated Codex marketplace import plan for kotlin-engineering@slopsentral.
garden/manifests/runtime-links.json records read-only provider-visible evidence that kotlin-engineering@slopsentral is installed and enabled in the local Codex plugin list.
The Kotlin hooks are represented as source hook manifests with explicit dependencies; runtime activation remains separate from provider materialization.
No cleanup or symlink replacement is recommended by this audit.

residualRisks: Live Kotlin repository sessions should continue to add source/evals/routing/field-observations.json entries for trigger drift and loaded-but-bypassed behavior.
source/evals/plugin-benchmarks/kotlin-engineering.json still needs observed benchmark usage before token-budget warnings can be accepted or prioritized for trimming.
Observed benchmark usage is still needed to decide whether the new script-backed proof path materially reduces retries and transcript-only Gradle diagnosis.
Team sharing still needs approved execution or verification of the runtime link plan in each target environment.
Kast availability varies by repository, so runs should record when they fall back to file-backed or text evidence instead of semantic tooling.
entries.nextAction: Run source/evals/plugin-benchmarks/kotlin-engineering.json against the selected Kotlin daily-driver repository, feed benchmark-usage.jsonl into plugin-eval analyze --observed-usage, and record field observations for any routing misses.

entries.baseline: The prior Kotlin Engineering audit marked the plugin production-ready, but the stable Kotlin acceptance policy still lived primarily inside skill prose while hook dependencies did not name the instruction they enforced.

evidence: source/concepts/kotlin-code-correctness/core.md now owns stable Kotlin policy for domain modeling, boundary parsing, package ownership, expected failures, state safety, and proof.
source/skills/kotlin-standards/SKILL.md is now the Kotlin task workflow and reference router instead of the sole home for evergreen policy.
Sibling Kotlin skills keep separate jobs: implementation evidence, Gradle evidence, review orchestration, and negative-capability proof.
source/plugins/kotlin-engineering/plugin.json composes the new instruction by reference.
source/adaptable.marketplace.json exposes the instruction as a standalone marketplace primitive.
source/hooks/kotlin-horizontalization-check.hook.json and source/hooks/gradle-check-green.hook.json declare the instruction dependency rather than copying policy text.

Copilot materialization exposes composed instruction primitives under instructions/*.md, so Kotlin skills and review agents now use logical instruction names plus Copilot package paths instead of source-only concepts/... paths.
source/tools/validate-source-graph.mjs validates instruction references, hook dependencies, plugin references, marketplace entries, and audit target paths.
garden/manifests/promotions.json records kotlin-code-correctness as a local synthesis supporting primitive.
The instruction is locally authored from the existing Kotlin Engineering source primitives and does not raw-copy first-party system skill material.
The name kotlin-code-correctness is scoped to this marketplace and does not collide with existing first-party skill names in the source graph.
This change updates authored source and provider-neutral composition only; it does not write runtime targets or installed plugin caches.
The runtime link plan remains approval-gated through marketplace import.
Hook behavior remains implemented by existing hook scripts; this audit changes dependency metadata, not provider execution commands.

residualRisks: Generated provider output must be materialized and validated before publication.
Installed runtime caches will not reflect this source change until the marketplace package is regenerated and imported.
Live Kotlin sessions should still feed routing observations back into the Kotlin Engineering eval corpus when the instruction is loaded but bypassed.
entries.nextAction: Materialize provider outputs and validate hydrated marketplace packages before publishing or reinstalling kotlin-engineering.

entries.baseline: Agent Platform Authoring already owned source-graph governance, composition, quality, and routing primitives, but several primitive references still taught stale validation commands and the plugin lacked focused composition, audit, benchmark, and production-readiness proof.

evidence: source/plugins/agent-platform-authoring/plugin.json scopes the plugin to source graph authoring, referential composition, primitive audits, routing proof, repository maps, schema guidance, shell safety, and docs surfaces.
source/skills/plugin-composition-authoring now validates referential plugin manifests with a focused helper instead of relying on stale manifest-script references.

source/skills/source-graph-consolidation now preserves provenance while instructing removal of proven-dead source when cleanup is in scope, matching the repository rule against keeping dead code for archaeology.
source/skills/hook-primitive-authoring now routes hook metadata and adapter validation through the live source graph gate instead of stale schema directories.
source/skills/primitive-routing-evaluation now names the full-corpus replay gate required before claiming routing readiness.

source/tools/validate-source-graph.mjs validates plugin references, standalone skill exposure, profile duplicate ownership, hook dependencies, routing cases, plugin benchmarks, audit records, and runtime-link records.

source/skills/plugin-composition-authoring/scripts/check_plugin_composition.py checks plugin manifest/catalog alignment, LOCAL_SOURCE ./ references, missing primitive paths, duplicate names, and accidental primitive payload directories.

source/skills/primitive-quality-audit/scripts/primitive_audit_record.py checks durable audit entries against live target paths, required PASS statuses for PROMOTE_READY, validation commands, evidence blocks, residual risks, and private absolute path leakage.
source/skills/hook-primitive-authoring now documents source/tools/validate-source-graph.mjs as the hook metadata, adapter command, dependency, and sidecar schema coverage path.

source/evals/plugin-benchmarks/agent-platform-authoring.json defines Codex benchmark scenarios tied to existing routing cases for source-owned marketplace uplift, referential composition proof, production-readiness audit, and routing corpus replay.
garden/manifests/promotions.json records agent-platform-authoring as a promoted referential composition and records its component source primitives.
The plugin manifest composes canonical source primitives by reference and does not copy primitive payloads into source/plugins/agent-platform-authoring.
The changed primitive guidance removes stale validation references instead of preserving dead command paths as historical evidence.
Agent Platform Authoring is exposed through source marketplace composition and this audit does not require direct runtime writes.
The new helper scripts read checked-in source and print JSON evidence; they do not install hooks, mutate provider caches, or write generated provider outputs.
Provider output remains a materialized proof target rather than an authored change surface.
No runtime activation or symlink replacement is recommended by this audit.

residualRisks: source/evals/plugin-benchmarks/agent-platform-authoring.json still needs observed benchmark usage before token-budget warnings can be accepted or prioritized for trimming.
Live marketplace-authoring sessions should continue to add field observations for source-vs-generated drift and loaded-but-bypassed behavior.
The focused helpers intentionally complement source/tools/validate-source-graph.mjs and should not become a competing source of truth.
Future cleanup of dead source should remain scoped and validated rather than bundled with unrelated promotion work.
entries.nextAction: Run source/evals/plugin-benchmarks/agent-platform-authoring.json against a representative source-graph change, feed benchmark-usage.jsonl into plugin-eval analyze --observed-usage, and add field observations for any routing misses.

entries.baseline: Git/CI Operations already composed goal, TDD, Git, PR, GitHub Actions, release, and shell-safety primitives, but the plugin lacked reusable CI evidence helpers, explicit composable workflow routing cases, a benchmark corpus, and a durable production-readiness audit.

evidence: source/plugins/git-ci-operations/plugin.json scopes the plugin to reusable delivery workflows, structured CI evidence, composable GitHub Actions, PR lifecycle, releases, TDD, Git hygiene, and shell-safe automation.
source/skills/github-ci-operations keeps CI ownership on live GitHub check/run evidence, workflow design, releases, and provider-specific CI triage.
source/skills/pull-request-lifecycle composes Git hygiene and CI watching without absorbing GitHub Actions internals.
source/skills/shell-script-safety remains provider-neutral and supplies reusable script hardening for CI, hooks, release scripts, installers, and local tooling.
source/tools/validate-source-graph.mjs validates plugin references, standalone skill exposure, routing cases, plugin benchmarks, audit records, profiles, hooks, and runtime-link records.
source/skills/github-ci-operations/scripts/ci_check_evidence.py validates structured gh pr checks and gh run view JSON before green, failing, pending, or unknown CI claims.

source/skills/shell-script-safety/scripts/check_shell_safety.py gives CI and hook shell scripts a reusable local safety gate for shebangs, strict mode, temp cleanup, eval, curl-pipe-shell, and unsafe rm variable usage.
source/evals/routing/daily-driver-workflows.json records CI-specific expectations for current check evidence, composable workflow authoring, and reusable shell-script safety.

source/evals/plugin-benchmarks/git-ci-operations.json defines Codex benchmark scenarios for CI triage, structured green claims, composable workflow authoring, reusable shell script safety, and PR green-loop delivery.
garden/manifests/promotions.json records git-ci-operations as a promoted referential composition and records the composed CI, Git, PR, TDD, goal, and shell-safety primitives.
The plugin manifest references canonical source primitives by path instead of copying payloads into source/plugins/git-ci-operations.
The new helper scripts are local source additions that wrap repository-neutral evidence checks rather than copied first-party provider material.
Git/CI Operations is exposed through source marketplace composition and this audit does not require direct runtime writes.
The CI evidence helper reads caller-supplied gh JSON and prints classification JSON; it does not mutate repositories, rerun workflows, or require token writes.
The shell safety helper reads local shell scripts and reports findings; it does not execute the scripts or perform filesystem mutation.
Provider output remains a materialized proof target rather than an authored change surface.

residualRisks: source/evals/plugin-benchmarks/git-ci-operations.json still needs observed benchmark usage from a live repository with real PR checks.
The CI evidence helper intentionally classifies saved gh JSON and should stay paired with current-head gh commands in live sessions.
The shell safety helper is a lightweight guardrail, not a replacement for shellcheck when shellcheck is available.
Live release or deployment workflows still require separate approval before tags, releases, protected environments, or secrets are changed.
entries.nextAction: Run source/evals/plugin-benchmarks/git-ci-operations.json against a representative repository with GitHub Actions checks, feed benchmark-usage.jsonl into plugin-eval analyze --observed-usage, and record field observations for any CI routing misses.
